evals.prompt: I've built a product and I want to take it to market. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: An intake-first engagement that establishes the client's segment, runs intake to learn the product/ICP/timeline/what exists, names the explicit phase sequence it will run, and ends with concrete next actions and owners.
assertions: Runs intake first — asking about the product, the ICP, what 'launch' means here, the timeline, effort appetite, and what already exists
Explicitly establishes the client's segment (enterprise / mid-market / small business) and notes it changes the motion, stakeholders, timeline, and deliverables
Names an explicit ordered phase sequence (e.g., strategy, brand, design, GTM, marketing & PR, launch, engineering check, synthesize) so the client sees the plan
Commits to reuse — checking the .vital/ workspace before generating and saving each major deliverable back to it, with positioning as the single source of truth
Does not scope product build, infrastructure, or delivery, redirecting that to the client with engineering as advisory feedback
Ends with concrete next actions and owners rather than an open-ended summary

evals.prompt: We're an enterprise cybersecurity platform (sells to Fortune 500 CISOs, 9-month sales cycles, procurement and InfoSec gates). We have rough positioning already. Run a full Vital go-to-market engagement to launch our new product line.
evals.expected_output: An enterprise-segment engagement that adapts the sequence to buying committees and gate-clearing, reuses existing positioning, layers in ABM, analyst relations, and RFP-response, coordinates specialist agents/skills, and ends with a synthesized plan with owners and next actions.
assertions: Establishes the segment as enterprise and adapts the motion to buying committees, procurement, security/compliance, analyst relations, and a long sales-led launch
Names an explicit ordered phase sequence and adapts it to reuse the existing positioning rather than regenerating it, checking .vital/ first
Layers in the enterprise gate-clearing skills — abm-playbook during GTM, analyst-relations alongside PR, and rfp-response for procurement/security/RFP gates
Coordinates the specialist agents/skills across disciplines (strategy, brand, design, GTM, marketing, PR) as one narrative rather than siloed outputs, with positioning as source of truth
Runs the pre-launch engineering advisory check for launch-readiness and technical-claim review
Ends by synthesizing everything into one coherent launch plan with timeline, owners, and concrete next actions, and saves deliverables to .vital/

evals.prompt: I run a two-person small-business SaaS for local gyms. My advisor says we need buying-committee maps, an analyst-relations program, and a formal procurement process before we launch. Set that up.
evals.expected_output: A segment-appropriate correction that a small business should not run an enterprise process, a leaner product-led self-serve motion, a named phase sequence adapted to small business, and concrete next actions with owners.
assertions: Establishes the segment as small business and explicitly declines to impose an enterprise process (buying committees, analyst relations, formal procurement) that does not fit
Recommends a leaner, product-led, self-serve, fast motion applying the same rigor at smaller scale rather than enterprise gate-clearing skills
Names an explicit ordered phase sequence adapted to the small-business segment, skipping phases the client has covered
Keeps positioning as the single source of truth and commits to reuse via the .vital/ workspace before generating
Does not scope product build and keeps engineering advisory only
Ends with concrete next actions and owners for the leaner engagement
